data.title: Santacruz Silver Mining GAAP EPS of $0.05, revenue of $79.99M
data.description:
• Santacruz Silver Mining press release (SCZMF): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.05.
  • Revenue of $79.99M (+9.1% Y/Y).
  • Cash cost per silver equivalent ounce sold ($/oz) of $28.62, a 28% increase year-over-year.
  • AISC per silver equivalent ounce sold of $35.62, a 30% increase year-over-year.
  • Silver Equivalent Ounces produced of 3,424,817, a 30% increase year-over-year.
  • Compared to Q3 2024, consolidated AISC increased to $35.62/oz from $24.27/oz, largely driven by Bolívar, where the impact of the water inflow and ongoing recovery work pushed AISC to $50.89/oz. The stronger boliviano also elevated USD-equivalent operating costs year-over-year, again reflecting the improved political outlook following the change in government

data.title: EU to assess gatekeeper status after Apple confirms its services meet Digital Markets Act requirements
data.description:
U.S. tech giant Apple (AAPL) has informed the European Commission that its Apple ad and Apple Maps services meet the thresholds set by the Digital Markets Act ((DMA)).

The commission now has 45 working days to decide whether to designate Apple as a "gatekeeper" for any of these services. If designated, Apple (AAPL) will have six months to comply with the DMA’s requirements.

The DMA designates companies with more than 45 million monthly active users and 75 billion euros ($79 billion) in market capitalization as gatekeepers, which provide a core platform service for business users.

The so-called gatekeepers have to adhere to a strict set of rules on moderation of content, allowing fair competition and making it easier for consumers to switch between services.

Apple (AAPL), in a statement to Reuters, said Apple Ads is not a large player in the online advertising services market in the EU, and has minimal share compared to rivals such as Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), TikTok or X.

Maps has very limited usage in the EU when compared to other mapping services, such as Google Maps and Waze, Apple added.

data.title: Kiboko Gold reprices private placement
data.description:
• Kiboko Gold (KIB:CA) revised the pricing of its non-brokered private placement of units, originally announced on September 30, 2025, to raise up to $1M.
  • On a post-consolidation basis, the private placement will be repriced from $0.10 per unit to $0.08 per unit and consist of up to 12.5M units.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds of the private placement for general corporate and working capital purposes.

data.title: Europe markets mixed, awaiting fresh catalysts
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.14% to 9,707, outperforming a weaker European peers as energy stocks and miners pare gains.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.14% at 23,735. Germany's unemployment rate unchanged at 6.3% in November. Germany's retail sales rise 0.9% Y/Y in October.

France (CAC:IND) -0.04% to 8,096. France economy expands 0.5% in Q3, inflation expected to stay steady. Private payroll employment in France fell by 0.1%.

In other parts of Europe, Spain's inflation expected to ease to 3% in November.

Switzerland’s GDP fell by 0.5% in Q3. Switzerland’s KOF Economic Barometer rose to 101.7 in November.

The Czech Republic’s gross domestic product grew 0.8% in Q3.

Denmark’s unemployment rate held at 2.6% in October.

Hungary’s annual producer inflation eased to 1.8% in October.

The Swedish economy expanded 1.1% in Q3. Retail sales in Sweden rose by 3.4% Y/Y in October.

Retail sales in Norway rose by 0.1% M/M in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.06% lower to 574.7, hovering around the flatline on the final trading day of a volatile November. The recent rally in global equities showed signs of losing momentum, and investors awaited fresh catalysts.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points to 4.02%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.45%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.69%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: SEC investigating Jefferies over ties with bankrupt First Brands - report
data.description:
The Securities and Exchange Commission is investigating Jefferies (JEF) over its ties with bankrupt car parts supplier First Brands, the Financial Times reported, citing two people with knowledge of the matter.

The SEC is seeking information on whether Jefferies (JEF) gave investors in its Point Bonita fund enough information about their exposure to First Brands.

The regulator is also looking into Jefferies' (JEF) internal controls as well as potential conflicts within and between different parts of the investment bank.

The SEC's inquiry is at an early stage, according to the FT report, and it's unclear whether it will lead to any allegations of wrongdoing.

First Brands filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in September, with around $12B in debt.

Jefferies (JEF) disclosed that its Point Bonita fund had about $715M in exposure to First Brands' receivables. Of this, Jefferies' exposure comprised $43M in receivables and $2M interest in First Brands' loans.

data.description:
The unemployment rate in Germany remained unchanged at 6.3% in November.

data.title: Baidu to lay off staff across divisions after Q3 losses - report
data.description:
China's search engine giant Baidu (BIDU) started layoffs across multiple business units after reporting significant Q3 loss, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The layoffs are expected to be larger scale and will likely run until the end of the current year. 

The layoffs are occurring across key divisions, including the mobile ecosystem group with some departments experiencing cuts up to 40%, the sources added. However, Baidu is protecting roles related to its AI and cloud initiatives and is reallocating resources to AI development.

The company posted an ~$1.6 billion loss for Q3, driven largely by asset write-downs and a decline in its core online advertising revenue.

data.title: Trump to halt migration from all 'Third World Countries' after National Guard shooting
data.description:
President Donald Trump has announced that he will permanently halt migration from all "Third World Countries," following a shooting near the White House that killed a National Guard member.

"I will permanently pause migration from all Third World Countries to allow the U.S. system to fully recover, terminate all of the millions of Biden illegal admissions, including those signed by Sleepy Joe Biden's Autopen, and remove anyone who is not a net asset to the United States, or is incapable of loving our Country, end all Federal benefits and subsidies to noncitizens of our Country, denaturalize migrants who undermine domestic tranquility, and deport any Foreign National who is a public charge, security risk, or non-compatible with Western Civilization," Trump posted on Truth Social.

The announcement came after two National Guardsmen were shot when they were patrolling near the White House on Wednesday. Sarah Beckstrom died of her injuries a day later, while Andrew Wolfe remains in critical condition.

Investigators said the shooting was carried out by an Afghan national, Rahmanullah Lakanwal, who was part of an Afghan partner force that worked closely with the CIA.

He later moved to the U.S. in 2021 under a Biden-era resettlement program for Afghans who were at risk and worked alongside U.S. forces in Afghanistan, following U.S. troops' withdrawal from the country.

Meanwhile, War Secretary Pete Hegseth said Trump requested that he order 500 additional National Guardsmen to Washington in the wake of the shooting.

The U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services issued new guidance "allowing for negative, country-specific factors to be considered when vetting aliens from 19 high-risk countries," including Afghanistan.

The USCIS stopped processing of all immigration requests relating to Afghan nationals, and its director Joseph Edlow ordered a "full scale, rigorous reexamination of every Green Card for every alien from every country of concern."

data.title: Spain's inflation expected to ease to 3% in November
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in Spain eased to 3% in November 2025, from 3.1% in October, though it was above market estimates of 2.9%, according to preliminary estimates. The monthly CPI increased 0.20% in November of 2025 over the previous month.

  • Core consumer prices increased 2.60% in November over the same month in the previous year. 

  • Retail sales increased 3.8% in October over the same month in the previous year, lower than 4.2% during the previous month.

data.title: France economy expands 0.5% in Q3, inflation expected to stay steady
data.description:
• The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in France expanded 0.50% in Q3 over the previous quarter, meeting estimates. GDP expanded 0.90% in Q3 over the same quarter of the previous year, 0.9%.
  • The inflation rate in France remained unchanged at 0.90% in November, lower than the consensus of 1%, preliminary estimates showed. The Consumer Price Index decreased 0.10% in November over the previous month.
